categories.title: Mountains & Peaks
categories.subtitle: Touch the sky at Africa's highest summits

attractions.title: Mount Kilimanjaro
attractions.description: Africa's tallest peak at 5,895m. The snow-capped free-standing volcano is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and the ultimate trekking destination.

attractions.title: Mount Meru
attractions.description: Tanzania's second-highest peak at 4,566m. A stunning volcanic cone offering spectacular views of Kilimanjaro and diverse wildlife encounters.

attractions.title: Ol Doinyo Lengai
attractions.description: The sacred 'Mountain of God' to the Maasai people. The only active natrocarbonatite volcano on Earth, rising 2,962m near Lake Natron.

attractions.title: Udzungwa Mountains
attractions.description: Biodiversity hotspot with endemic species, lush rainforests, and stunning waterfalls. Known as the 'Galapagos of Africa'.

categories.title: Wildlife & Safari
categories.subtitle: Witness the greatest wildlife spectacle on Earth

attractions.title: Serengeti National Park
attractions.description: Home to the Great Migration — over 1.5 million wildebeest and 250,000 zebra cross the plains annually. Africa's most iconic safari destination.

attractions.title: Ngorongoro Crater
attractions.description: The world's largest intact volcanic caldera, hosting 25,000+ large animals including the Big Five in a breathtaking natural amphitheatre.

attractions.title: Tarangire National Park
attractions.description: Famous for enormous elephant herds, ancient baobab trees, and over 550 bird species. A hidden gem of the northern safari circuit.

attractions.title: Selous Game Reserve
attractions.description: Africa's largest protected wildlife area, offering boat safaris on the Rufiji River and encounters with rare African wild dogs.

categories.title: Valleys & Plains
categories.subtitle: Explore the vast landscapes of the Great Rift Valley

attractions.title: Great Rift Valley
attractions.description: A geological wonder stretching across Tanzania, featuring dramatic escarpments, alkaline lakes, and breathtaking panoramic vistas.

attractions.title: Olduvai Gorge
attractions.description: The 'Cradle of Mankind' — one of the world's most important paleoanthropological sites where early human fossils were discovered.

attractions.title: Usambara Mountains Valley
attractions.description: Rolling green hills with tea plantations, misty forests, and stunning viewpoints. A paradise for hikers and nature lovers.

attractions.title: Lake Victoria
attractions.description: Africa's largest lake and the world's second-largest freshwater lake. Rich in fishing culture and home to diverse birdlife.

attractions.title: Lake Tanganyika
attractions.description: The world's second-deepest and longest freshwater lake, hosting over 350 species of fish. Crystal-clear waters perfect for diving.

attractions.title: Lake Natron
attractions.description: A surreal crimson-colored alkaline lake and the primary breeding ground for millions of lesser flamingos. An otherworldly landscape.

attractions.title: Rufiji River
attractions.description: Tanzania's largest river flowing through the Selous, offering spectacular boat safaris with hippos, crocodiles, and rich birdlife.

categories.title: Waterfalls
categories.subtitle: Feel the thunder of cascading tropical waters

attractions.title: Kalambo Falls
attractions.description: Africa's second-highest uninterrupted waterfall at 221m, plunging into Lake Tanganyika. An archaeological treasure with Stone Age artefacts.

attractions.title: Materuni Waterfalls
attractions.description: A spectacular 70m cascade nestled in the lush foothills of Kilimanjaro. Swim in the crystal pool and visit Chagga coffee farms.

attractions.title: Sanje Waterfalls
attractions.description: A breathtaking 170m waterfall in the Udzungwa Mountains, cascading through pristine rainforest inhabited by rare primates.

categories.title: Historical Sites
categories.subtitle: Journey through Tanzania's rich cultural heritage

attractions.title: Stone Town, Zanzibar
attractions.description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site with labyrinthine alleys, ornate carved doors, grand palaces, and a fusion of Arab, Persian, Indian and European influences.

attractions.title: Kilwa Kisiwani Ruins
attractions.description: Once the greatest trading city on the East African coast. The 13th-century ruins include the Great Mosque, one of the earliest in sub-Saharan Africa.

attractions.title: Bagamoyo Historic Town
attractions.description: A former slave-trade port and the first capital of German East Africa. Its crumbling colonial buildings and caravan routes tell stories of resilience.

attractions.title: Kondoa Rock Art Sites
attractions.description: A UNESCO World Heritage collection of over 150 rock shelters with paintings spanning 2,000+ years, depicting hunting scenes, rituals, and daily life.
